name: Xiezhi
description: The Xiezhi (獬豸) is a mythical creature in Chinese mythology, known for its innate ability to distinguish right from wrong and its unwavering commitment to justice. Often depicted as a single-horned goat or ox-like creature, it is considered a symbol of law and order, fairness, and integrity. The Xiezhi is believed to possess a keen intellect and an unyielding moral compass, enabling it to identify the guilty and punish them accordingly. Its horn is said to be capable of piercing the hearts of the wicked, ensuring that justice prevails. In ancient China, the Xiezhi was a popular motif in legal and governmental settings, adorning the robes of judges and other officials to symbolize their dedication to upholding the law. Its presence served as a constant reminder of the importance of impartiality and the consequences of corruption. The Xiezhi embodies the ideals of a just and equitable society, representing the hope that truth will always triumph over falsehood and that the guilty will be held accountable for their actions. It’s a powerful symbol of righteousness, and its image continues to be revered in Chinese culture as a testament to the enduring pursuit of justice. The Xiezhi is also sometimes associated with fire, adding another layer of symbolism to its already complex representation.
subtitle: The Divine Judge of Right and Wrong
appearance: The Xiezhi is typically depicted with a body resembling a goat or ox, covered in thick fur. It possesses a single, prominent horn on its forehead, which is its primary weapon for punishing the guilty. Its eyes are said to be sharp and perceptive, capable of discerning the truth from lies. The color of its fur varies in depictions, ranging from black to white or even a fiery red, depending on the artistic interpretation. Some depictions also include scales or other reptilian features, further emphasizing its unique and mythical nature. Its overall appearance is imposing and dignified, reflecting its role as a symbol of justice and authority.
habitat: The Xiezhi is primarily a mythical creature, existing in the realm of legends and folklore. It is often associated with courtrooms, temples dedicated to justice, and other places where the law is upheld. It may also be found in remote mountains or forests, where it roams in search of evildoers to punish. Its presence is not tied to a specific geographical location, but rather to the concept of justice and the pursuit of righteousness.
behavior: The Xiezhi is characterized by its unwavering commitment to justice and its intolerance of wrongdoing. It is said to be calm and composed until it encounters someone who is guilty of a crime, at which point it becomes fiercely determined to bring them to justice. The Xiezhi is not motivated by malice or revenge, but rather by a deep-seated sense of fairness and a desire to uphold the law. It is a solitary creature, preferring to operate independently and relying on its own judgment to discern the truth.

keyMyths.title: The Xiezhi and the Corrupt Official
keyMyths.description: A corrupt official attempted to bribe the Xiezhi with riches, hoping to escape punishment for his crimes. However, the Xiezhi, true to its nature, rejected the bribe and used its horn to expose the official's misdeeds, bringing him to justice.

sources.author: Werner, E.T.C.
sources.description: A comprehensive collection of Chinese myths and legends, including information about the Xiezhi.
sources.text: A comprehensive collection of Chinese myths and legends, including information about the Xiezhi.
